get.description:
Redirect the user to the SSO authorization endpoint.

After the user authenticates, they are redirected back to
``/auth/sso/callback`` with an authorization code which is
exchanged for access + refresh tokens.

parameters.name: redirect_after

schema.description: URL to redirect to after successful sign-in
schema.title: Redirect After

parameters.description: URL to redirect to after successful sign-in

post.summary: SSO login — credentials → tokens or 2FA challenge
post.description:
Proxy login to the SSO server.

Expects JSON body: ``{"email": "...", "password": "..."}``

The SSO server returns tokens directly (when 2FA is disabled)
or a ``login_token`` for the 2FA challenge.

200.description:
**Dual response** depending on user's 2FA setting:

- **2FA disabled** → tokens with `access_token`, `refresh_token`, `expires_in`
- **2FA enabled** → `two_factor_required`, `login_token`, `email`, `expires_in` — follow up with `POST /auth/sso/login/verify-2fa`
